title: The Static Kitchen

features.name: recipe turntable
features.desc-on-touch: a carousel of standing recipe cards, each glitching through permutations of ingredients, as if the kitchen is computing what hunger might taste like

features.name: file mixer
features.desc-on-touch: a kitchen mixer reassembled from directory listings, its spinning geometry folding data into something solid, beaters cutting static into instruction

features.name: counter glitch
features.desc-on-touch: the work surface where recipes appear and vanish, where your hands are always reaching for something that just became something else

features.name: timing bell
features.desc-on-touch: a bell from nowhere that rings in algorithms instead of sound, telling you when you've finished cooking something you never started

features.name: extraction hood
features.desc-on-touch: industrial venting that pulls upward, extracting not smoke but meaning—the archive consuming its own hunger as it's being created

features.name: storage manifest
features.desc-on-touch: a floating directory, every shelf documented, every ingredient cross-referenced, the kitchen remembering what it is supposed to be making

inscription: you are not cooking. the kitchen is cooking itself through what it remembers of hunger

file.name: Recipe_Compilation_Archive_CORRUPTED.txt
file.note: instruction manuals from the food court, ingredients listed recursively, methods that reference themselves, checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable

exits.label: back out through the serving counter, into neon false daylight
deeper into preparation, where recipes are stored as pure mathematics
into storage chambers where ingredients wait in the cold dark
